name: George Gelnovatch
bio: George Gelnovatch is the most decorated active head coach in US college soccer. He has been at Virginia since 1995 — 30 years — building the winningest program in NCAA history. His coaching tree includes Aidan Morris (Columbus Crew, USMNT), Claudio Reyna (USMNT captain), John Harkes, and Tony Meola — players who define the history of American soccer. Gelnovatch is known for a high-pressing, technically sophisticated system that mirrors European club football. He prepares players for professional careers more than any other active college coach. His playing style perfectly suits a composed, box-to-box 8 with range and pressing intensity.

staff.name: Chris Gentile
staff.bg: Former UVA player. Manages all international recruiting. Key contact for Australian players.

staff.name: Tanner Koziol
staff.bg: Former professional player. Handles GK and defensive organisation.

staff.name: Danny Cruz
staff.bg: Former Philadelphia Union MLS coach. Brings elite professional analysis systems.

name: Todd Yeagley
record: Big Ten champs 2020, 2021, 2023, 2024. NCAA Tournament 4 consecutive years.
bio: Todd Yeagley is the son of legendary Indiana coach Jerry Yeagley and has maintained the Hoosiers as the most decorated program in US soccer history. Under him, Indiana won Big Ten titles in 2020, 2021, 2023, and 2024. His 2021 team developed Aidan Morris who became a Columbus Crew starter and USMNT regular within 2 years of graduating. His 2025 team sent Jansen Miller to Sporting Kansas City in Round 1. Yeagley runs a high-intensity pressing system with intricate positional play — the most tactically demanding college program in the Big Ten. The School of Public Health's 9-lab kinesiology facility (including the only bioenergetics lab of its kind in the USA) is directly adjacent to the soccer programme.

staff.name: Ben Yeagley
staff.bg: Jerry Yeagley's grandson. Former Indiana player. Deep system knowledge.

staff.name: Austin da Luz
staff.bg: Former USMNT player and MLS midfielder. Brings firsthand elite professional experience to training.

staff.name: Zachary Smith
staff.bg: School of Kinesiology integration — leads bioenergetics testing for player load management.

name: Ryan Jorden
record: 13 MLS draft picks. NCAA Tournament 4 years running. Pac-12 Champs 2023, Big Ten Tourn 2024.
bio: Ryan Jorden joined UCLA in 2018 and has transformed the program into a consistent Big Ten contender following the conference expansion. His coaching philosophy centres on technical excellence and player development — 13 of his players have been drafted by MLS teams during his tenure. He has produced Ahmed Longmire (#10 overall 2022, Nashville SC), Grayson Doody (R1 2024, CF Montréal), and Nicholas Cavallo (R3 2025). Jorden's approach blends physical athleticism with technical precision — a style that develops complete midfielders. UCLA's proximity to LAFC and LA Galaxy training facilities gives players extraordinary access to professional environments.

staff.name: Jimmy Villa
staff.bg: Former UCLA player and MLS veteran. One of the most respected assistant coaches in D1.

staff.name: Eddie Lewis
staff.bg: Former LA Galaxy and Portland Timbers MLS star. USMNT career. Brings elite professional mentality and recruiting connections.

staff.name: Xavier Gomez
staff.bg: Former professional GK. Works with both GK corps and trains outfield shot-stopping.

name: Tim Vom Steeg
record: 350+ career wins. NCAA 2006 title. Big West Champs 2021, 2022, 2024.
bio: Tim Vom Steeg is one of the most experienced and respected coaches in D1 college soccer. With 350+ career wins at UCSB, he has built one of the most consistent programs in the Big West. His development of Manu Duah — the #1 overall pick in the 2025 MLS Draft, the highest any Big West player has ever been selected — is his career masterpiece. Vom Steeg's system emphasises technical quality and tactical positioning, with a high rate of player development into professional environments. His coaching longevity (22+ years at one institution) signals deep knowledge of what the UCSB academic and athletic environment offers to players.

staff.name: Josue Espinoza
staff.bg: Former UCSB player. Lead recruiter for international talent. Latin American network strong.

staff.name: Brian Kelly
staff.bg: Former professional GK. Works goalkeeping and assists with set piece design.

name: Kyle Russell
record: 2021 CUSA Coach of Year. AAC Champs 2022. AAC Final 2024. 5 conference finals in 6 seasons.
bio: Kyle Russell is the standout young developer coach in this guide. In 6 seasons at FIU he has taken the program from CUSA to AAC and won the conference in 2022, reached the championship match in 2024, and sent Stephen Afrifa to Sporting Kansas City as the #8 overall pick in 2022. Michael Appiah joined Inter Miami in 2025 — a direct connection to the MLS team in FIU's backyard. Russell's pressing-intensive, technically demanding system is modelled on European club football. He has exceptional recruitment instinct and has built the FIU program into a genuine AAC powerhouse that is transitioning into the ACC in 2026.

staff.name: TBD — verify with FIU Athletics
staff.bg: FIU runs a lean staff model. Verify current assistants directly with athletics department.

staff.name: Jose Marin
staff.bg: Works GK development and set piece. Previously at Miami-area lower league clubs.

name: Kevin Hudson
record: 2025 ACC Tournament Champions — first in SMU history. Elite Eight 2024. Ranked #5 nationally 2020.
bio: Kevin Hudson has built SMU into an ACC-calibre program before SMU even officially joined the conference. His 2025 team won the ACC Tournament — the first time in program history — and his pipeline has produced Jaylinn Mitchell (2026 R1 MLS), Garrett McLaughlin (#8 overall 2020), and Hermann Trophy winner Eddie Munjoma (FC Dallas HGP). Luchi Gonzalez — the current FC Dallas head coach — is an SMU alum, creating a direct professional network from the SMU campus to the MLS club in the same city. Hudson runs a possession-based pressing system that would develop Olivier's range passing and positional intelligence.

staff.name: Christian Martinez
staff.bg: Former SMU player. Handles midfielder development and video analysis.

staff.name: Alden Sierakowski
staff.bg: Manages domestic and international recruiting. Key relationship with FC Dallas academy.

staff.name: Rodrigo Flores
staff.bg: Former professional GK. FC Dallas connections used for player placements.

name: George Kiefer
record: 4 conf titles, 10 NCAA appearances (first tenure). 2025 Far West Region Staff of Year at GCU.
bio: George Kiefer is one of the most accomplished coaches in USF history — his first tenure at the Bulls produced 4 conference titles and 10 NCAA Tournament appearances. He left to become head coach at Grand Canyon University, where his 2025 staff won the Far West Region Staff of the Year award. His return to USF in December 2025 is the biggest coaching story in AAC soccer. Kiefer is a possession-first builder who creates deep tactical awareness in his midfielders. His return signals institutional commitment to rebuilding USF soccer to its former AAC prominence. This is a ground-floor opportunity to join a program on the way up under a proven builder.

staff.name: Staff being rebuilt — verify with USF Athletics
staff.bg: Full staff announcement expected early 2026. Platform Sports Management should confirm.

name: Jim Barlow
record: 237-184-72. 7 Ivy League titles. 2025 Northeast Regional Coaching Staff of Year.
bio: Jim Barlow is in his 29th season at Princeton and has made 2024–2025 the best back-to-back seasons in program history. His 2025 team completed the first-ever Ivy League undefeated regular season + Tournament double in history. Barlow has coached 5 MLS players in his tenure and was named 2025 Northeast Regional Coaching Staff of the Year. As a Princeton alum himself, he has a deep connection to the academic environment and has consistently developed players who become leaders in professional and business careers. His tactical system focuses on intelligent positional play and pressing — ideal for an 8/10.

staff.name: Patrick DePace
staff.bg: Former Princeton player. Defensive organisation and set pieces. Long tenure with Barlow.

staff.name: Adam Faber
staff.bg: Recruiting focus. Has developed relationships with international recruiting platforms.

name: Bobby Muuss
record: 4 MLS picks in single 2025 draft — highest single-year rate in ACC history for the program.
bio: Bobby Muuss has built Wake Forest into a consistent ACC contender with one of the best single-season MLS draft outputs in recent history — 4 picks in the 2025 draft alone (Travis Smith Jr R1 Chicago Fire, Liam O'Gara R3, Trace Alphin R2 LA Galaxy). His possession-based system focuses on technical quality and tactical awareness, making Wake Forest a genuine developer of midfielders. The ACC competition ensures players are tested against the highest standard weekly. Wake Forest Baptist Medical Centre proximity provides unique clinical exposure for pre-PT students. Small campus (8,500) means strong personal coaching relationships.

staff.name: Brad Lyons
staff.bg: Former Wake Forest player. Midfielder specialist — directly relevant for Olivier development.

staff.name: Chris Konopka
staff.bg: Former professional GK (multiple MLS clubs). Brings professional standards to conditioning.

name: Lucas Martorana
record: 61-9-6 record as assistant before becoming HC. 2025 SSC Regular Season Champions. #2 nationally.
bio: Lucas Martorana is the most impressive young head coach in D2 soccer. With a 61-9-6 record as assistant before taking the HC role in 2022, he led PBA to the SSC Regular Season title in 2025 (8-0-2 conference record) and finished ranked #2 nationally. His 2025 squad featured All-American Hermoso at the back and Tin Vastic as SSC leading scorer. Martorana is a former USL/PDL/MISL professional player — he brings firsthand professional experience to everything he coaches. The combination of his development approach and PBA's unique Pre-Physical Therapy degree concentration makes this arguably the best overall package in the guide for Olivier's specific goals.

staff.name: Carlos Murillo
staff.bg: Former USL professional. Colombian-American coach with strong Latin American recruiting network.

staff.name: TBD — verify for full staff list
staff.bg: PBA runs a lean staff. Additional staff to confirm with athletics.

name: Brian Young
record: LSC Coach of Year. Multiple LSC Championship appearances. NCAA D2 Tournament qualifier.
bio: Brian Young is the head coach of the St. Edward University men soccer program in Austin, Texas. Long-tenured HC who has built the program in the Lone Star Conference. Associate HC Corey Miller has been on staff since 2017. Program has Austin FC development connections.

staff.name: Omar Sanchez
staff.bg: Former LSC player. Midfielder specialist with Austin Bold FC (USL2) connections.

name: John Rootes
record: 194-54-31 at Lynn (2008-2025). 3x NCAA D2 National Champion (2012, 2014, 2024). United Soccer Coaches National Staff of Year 2024.
bio: John Rootes has been head coach at Lynn University since 2008 — the most successful coach in program history. Three-time NCAA Division II National Champion (2012, 2014, 2024), he has built Lynn into a perennial D2 powerhouse. His 2024 team went undefeated (20-0-2) to claim the national title. Named United Soccer Coaches National Staff of Year 2024. Has coached 26 All-Americans and developed multiple international players including Australians. His systems-based approach and European recruiting network makes Lynn particularly attractive for international players.

staff.name: Victor Norat
staff.bg: Venezuelan-American coach. Latin American and international recruiting specialist.

staff.name: TBD
staff.bg: Verify current full staff with Lynn Athletics.

name: Petsa Ivanovic
record: Multiple SSC Championships. US Soccer Federation licensed. Program: 4 all-time D2 NCAA titles.
bio: Petsa Ivanovic coaches at the most historically decorated D2 program in the country — Barry's 4 national championships (1984, 1986, 1993, 2001) are an all-time D2 record. He is a USSF licensed coach who brings a professional standards approach to the program. Barry's School of Human Performance is a dedicated college with explicit pre-PT curriculum (HPS 450 Pre-Physical Therapy Seminar is a formal degree requirement — rare at D2 level). Miami Shores location provides multicultural energy that mirrors Sydney, and Inter Miami CF connections offer occasional pro pathway opportunities.

staff.name: TBD
staff.bg: Verify current staff with Barry Athletics.

staff.name: Luis Torrealba
staff.bg: Venezuelan-American coach. Former professional GK.

name: Billy Martin
record: Multiple NAIA national tournament appearances. Specific Australian player professional placements.
bio: Billy Martin is the head coach of the Oklahoma City University men and women soccer programs since 2020. Martin is an OCU alumnus who served as assistant to legendary founder coach Brian Harvey for 17 years (1996-2014) before becoming HC. Harvey coached from 1986 until his retirement in 2019 and passed away in January 2023. Harvey played professionally in Australia, China, England and USA - this was his personal history, not a current program specialty. NAIA scholarship flexibility makes OCU financially accessible. Oklahoma City has a growing soccer community.

staff.name: TBD
staff.bg: Verify with OCU Athletics. Small NAIA staff model.

name: Dave Masur
record: 450+ career wins. Multiple Big East appearances. Experienced with international players.
bio: Dave Masur has been at St. John's for over 30 years and has 450+ career wins — one of the most experienced active head coaches in D1 soccer. His program at Queens NYC sits within the most extraordinary clinical network in the world: Hospital for Special Surgery (ranked #1 orthopaedic hospital globally), NYU Langone, and NY Presbyterian are all accessible for DPT observation hours. Masur has extensive experience with international players — St. John's diverse student body means Australian players won't feel isolated. Big East competition is genuine D1 elite level. NYC soccer culture includes NYCFC and Red Bulls, adding professional exposure.

staff.name: Eric Nulan
staff.bg: Former D1 player. Handles tactical video and defensive shape.

staff.name: TBD
staff.bg: Verify current staff with St. John's Athletics.

name: Keith Wiggans
record: 1-4-3 (2020) improved to 10-6-2 (2024). CofC alum. Played 7 professional seasons for Charleston Battery.
bio: Keith Wiggans is both a College of Charleston alumnus and former professional player for the Charleston Battery (USL), having played 7 seasons for the local club. This connection to the professional game in Charleston gives his program authentic links to the local professional soccer ecosystem. His turnaround at CofC is impressive — from a 1-win season in 2020 to 10 wins in 2024 — demonstrating consistent program building. Charleston is one of America's most beautiful historic cities with a warm coastal climate. Wiggans' personal story (alum, pro, then coach) creates a motivational environment for players who aspire to professional careers.

staff.name: TBD
staff.bg: Verify with CofC Athletics. CAA-level support staff.
